generated_oml:
import "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/INTO-CPS-Association/example-incubator/main/vocab/DTDFVocab.owl" as DTDFVocab
instance Heatbed : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Heating element actuator for thermal incubator system, part of physical acting components"
]
instance Fan : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Fan for air circulation in thermal incubator system, part of physical acting components"
]
instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "External temperature sensor for ambient temperature measurement"
    DTDFVocab:producedData AmbientTemperatureDataTransmitted
]
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "First internal temperature sensor for thermal incubator"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted1
]
instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Second internal temperature sensor for thermal incubator"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted2
]
instance RaspberryPi : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Hardware platform for controller implementation in thermal incubator system"
]
instance AmbientTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]
instance InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]
instance InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]
instance AmbientTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw ambient temperature data from external sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData AmbientTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterEnabler
]
instance InternalTemperatureData1 : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw internal temperature data from first sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted1
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterEnabler
]
instance InternalTemperatureData2 : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw internal temperature data from second sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted2
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterEnabler
]
instance AveragedInternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Calculated internal temperature as average of T2 and T3 readings"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted1, InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted2
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterEnabler
]
instance ModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Thermal simulation model with 2 free parameters, incorporating all four assumptions: uniform temperature distribution, no wall heat accumulation, constant specific heat capacity of air, and efficient energy transfer from heatbed to air"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterEnabler
]
instance ModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Thermal simulation model with 4 free parameters, relaxing Assumption 4 to account for heatbed thermal inertia"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterEnabler
]
instance RabbitMQEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source message broker for inter-component communication"
    DTDFVocab:enables BangBangControllerService, StateEstimationService
]
instance KalmanFilterEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Kalman filter algorithm for state estimation of temperature dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:enables StateEstimationService
]
instance GitHubEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Public GitHub repository for version control of digital twin components"
    DTDFVocab:enables DevelopmentService
]
instance BangBangControllerService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Bang-bang controller service for thermal regulation with LL, UL, H, and C parameters"
    DTDFVocab:provides HeatbedActivationAction, HeatbedDeactivationAction, FanActivationAction, FanDeactivationAction
]
instance StateEstimationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "State estimation service using Kalman filter for temperature dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:provides EstimatedTemperatureInsight
]
instance DevelopmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Version control and code management service for digital twin development"
    DTDFVocab:provides VersionControlAction
]
instance HeatbedActivationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to activate heatbed heating element"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance HeatbedDeactivationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to deactivate heatbed heating element"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance FanActivationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to activate air circulation fan"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance FanDeactivationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to deactivate air circulation fan"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance VersionControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action for version control of digital twin code and specifications"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance EstimatedTemperatureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Estimated internal temperature from Kalman filter state estimation"
]
instance ThermalIncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Styrofoam-insulated container for temperature regulation in thermal incubator system"
    base:contains Heatbed, Fan, ExternalTemperatureSensor, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, RaspberryPi
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands for heatbed and fan are transmitted via RabbitMQ from digital twin components to the controller. The controller processes these commands and sends them to the low-level driver, which enforces a safe protocol for execution. Command validation occurs at the low-level driver to ensure safe operation, and feedback on command execution status is relayed back through RabbitMQ for monitoring."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system utilizes RabbitMQ as a message broker for communication between the physical twin's controller and low-level driver components. Digital twin components listen to these messages to synchronize data, incorporating Model A and Model B for temperature simulation and calibration as described in Section 3.3. The architecture is structured around this communication flow, as illustrated in Figure 7."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model validation includes testing Assumption 1 (uniform temperature distribution) in section 4. Calibration results (Figure 13) indicate Model B better predicts thermal inertia. Uncertainty quantification and error correction mechanisms are not explicitly described in the documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Communication between physical and digital components uses RabbitMQ as a message broker with a broker-based network topology. However, specific details on bandwidth, latency requirements, redundancy mechanisms, and security protocols are not provided in the documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The system is deployed on edge hardware (Raspberry Pi) running the controller, low-level driver, and RabbitMQ message broker on-premise. Computational requirements, scalability mechanisms, and cloud deployment options are not detailed in the documents."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration is achieved using RabbitMQ (AMQP protocol) as the messaging backbone for data exchange between the physical twin's low-level driver, controller, and digital twin components. The system's open-source implementation is hosted on GitHub (https://github.com/INTO-CPS-Association/example-incubator), providing standardized interfaces for external system integration."
]
